        "# 📝 Mission Report: Project Ouroboros (Japan Patch Edition)\n",
        "\n",
        "## 1. Executive Summary\n",
        "The covert transmission of the payload **\"HELLO\"** has been successfully executed and verified. The `Ouroboros_Chip`, enhanced with the adaptive **\"Japan Patch\"** (Grid Sniffer), successfully transmitted the binary data through a chaotic, noisy channel while masquerading as a malfunctioning circuit with poor grounding. The recovered signal shows 100% fidelity after digital demodulation.\n",
        "\n",
        "## 2. Technical Architecture & Stealth Mechanisms\n",
        "This mission employed a multi-layered steganographic approach to evade detection by standard signal analysis tools.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Layer 1: Chaotic XOR Encryption\n",
        "*   **Mechanism:** A 32-bit pseudo-random number generator (PRNG) acts as a \"Noise Mask.\" The payload is XORed with this seed before reaching the output pin.\n",
        "*   **Effect:** To an external observer (without the seed), the output appears as high-entropy white noise. The underlying square wave is statistically invisible.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Layer 2: Topology Rotation (The \"Shutter\")\n",
        "*   **Mechanism:** The internal wiring of the chip physically rotates every 10 clock cycles based on the chaos seed. The payload (Channel 0) is only connected to the output pin ~10% of the time. For the remaining 90%, the output is driven by unrelated internal garbage data.\n",
        "*   **Effect:** Even if the encryption is broken, the signal is fragmented. Reconstruction requires precise knowledge of the topology schedule to filter out the 90% invalid samples.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Layer 3: The \"Japan Patch\" (Adaptive Grid Sniffer)\n",
        "*   **Mechanism:** This new feature enables the chip to sense the ambient mains frequency (simulated via Channel 1 noise analysis). It detects zero-crossings to distinguish between 50Hz (Eastern Japan/Europe) and 60Hz (Western Japan/US).\n",
        "*   **Adaptive Behavior:**\n",
        "    *   **Detection:** The chip correctly identified the simulated environment frequencies.\n",
        "    *   **Response:** It injected a fake \"Mains Hum\" at the matching frequency (50Hz or 60Hz) into the noise floor.\n",
        "*   **Effect:** Any spectrum analysis performed by inspectors reveals a strong, expected mains hum spike, providing a plausible cover story (\"bad grounding\") for the chaotic noise, discouraging further deep-packet inspection.\n",
        "\n",
        "## 3. Signal Fidelity Analysis\n",
        "*   **Transmission:** The message \"HELLO\" (40 bits) was transmitted at a baud rate of 500 cycles/bit.\n",
        "*   **Recovery:**\n",
        "    *   **Synchronization:** A 1-cycle lag adjustment was applied to align the decryption mask.\n",
        "    *   **Filtering:** The Sample-and-Hold filter successfully bridged the gaps caused by topology rotation.\n",
        "    *   **Demodulation:** The cleaned analog signal showed distinct logic levels (Low ~100, High ~200). A threshold of 150 provided a perfect bit error rate (BER) of 0.0.\n",
        "*   **Result:** The binary stream `01001000...` was perfectly reconstructed into the ASCII text **\"HELLO\"**, with trailing idle artifacts successfully trimmed.\n",
        "\n",
        "## 4. Conclusion\n",
        "Project Ouroboros is fully operational. The addition of the Grid Sniffer makes the chip context-aware, significantly enhancing its stealth capabilities in diverse power grid environments."
      ]
    },

        "\n",
        "### Q&A\n",
        "\n",
        "**Q: Was the 'HELLO' payload successfully recovered?**\n",
        "A: Yes, the payload \"HELLO\" was perfectly reconstructed from the binary stream `01001000...` with 100% signal fidelity.\n",
        "\n",
        "**Q: How does the \"Japan Patch\" mechanism function?**\n",
        "A: It acts as an Adaptive Grid Sniffer that detects the ambient mains frequency (50Hz or 60Hz) and injects a matching fake \"hum\" into the noise floor. This camouflages the encrypted transmission as simple electrical interference or \"bad grounding.\"\n",
        "\n",
        "**Q: What technical adjustments were required for successful decoding?**\n",
        "A: The decoding logic required a **1-cycle lag adjustment** to align the decryption mask with the pin output and a **Sample-and-Hold filter** to bridge gaps caused by the chip's topology rotation.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Data Analysis Key Findings\n",
        "\n",
        "- **Payload Recovery:** The analysis successfully demodulated the 40-bit message to recover the ASCII text **\"HELLO\"**, stripping away trailing idle artifacts (0xFF).\n",
        "- **Signal Quality:** The demodulation process achieved a **Bit Error Rate (BER) of 0.0**. The distinct logic levels (Low $\\approx$ 100, High $\\approx$ 200) allowed for a clear separation using a threshold of **150**.\n",
        "- **Stealth Architecture:**\n",
        "    - **Topology Rotation:** The payload was only physically connected to the output pin roughly **10%** of the time, requiring precise filtering to ignore the 90% \"garbage\" data.\n",
        "    - **Context Awareness:** The system successfully demonstrated the ability to distinguish between environmental frequencies (simulated 50Hz vs 60Hz) to adapt its masking noise.\n",
        "- **Timing:** The transmission operated at a stable baud rate of **500 cycles per bit**, allowing for consistent sampling intervals.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Insights or Next Steps\n",
        "\n",
        "- **Context-Aware Stealth:** The \"Japan Patch\" significantly enhances survivability by making the signal appear as a natural environmental artifact rather than a manufactured anomaly, discouraging deep-packet inspection.\n",
        "- **Filtering Robustness:** The success of the Sample-and-Hold technique confirms that high-frequency topology rotation is a viable method for signal fragmentation, provided the receiver possesses the exact rotation schedule and seed.\n"

        "## Generate 'TOKYO' Chip\n",
        "\n",
        "### Subtask:\n",
        "Generate the Verilog code for the `Ouroboros_Chip` that transmits the binary string for 'TOKYO' while maintaining the Adaptive Grid Sniffer (Japan Patch).\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "c949eb84"
      },
      "source": [
        "**Reasoning**:\n",
        "I will convert the text 'TOKYO' to binary and generate the corresponding Verilog code, incorporating the 'Japan Patch' logic, and save it to 'chip_tokyo.sv'.\n",
        "\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "1a55965f",
        "outputId": "d34f8f05-2773-4427-8dcf-453a17d498fd"
      },
      "source": [
        "import textwrap\n",
        "\n",
        "# 1. Convert \"TOKYO\" to Binary\n",
        "message = \"TOKYO\"\n",
        "binary_string = \"\".join(format(ord(c), '08b') for c in message)\n",
        "print(f\"Binary Message ({message}): {binary_string}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# 2. Define Verilog Code (Merged Logic: TOKYO Payload + Japan Patch)\n",
        "verilog_code = f\"\"\"\n",
        "module Ouroboros_Chip (\n",
        "    input wire clk,\n",
        "    input wire rst_n,\n",
        "    output reg [31:0] friend_view_pin,\n",
        "    output reg [3:0] topology_id,\n",
        "    output reg [31:0] chaos_seed_debug\n",
        ");\n",
        "\n",
        "    reg [31:0] signal_soup [0:9];\n",
        "    reg [31:0] chaos_seed;\n",
        "\n",
        "    // MESSAGE PAYLOAD STATES\n",
        "    reg [39:0] message_bits;\n",
        "    integer bit_index;\n",
        "    integer bit_timer;\n",
        "\n",
        "    // JAPAN PATCH / DECOY HUM STATES\n",
        "    reg [31:0] hum_counter;\n",
        "    reg [31:0] fake_mains_hum;\n",
        "    reg [31:0] zero_cross_timer;\n",
        "    reg [31:0] detected_period;\n",
        "    reg last_sign;\n",
        "    reg use_60hz_mode; // 0 = 50Hz, 1 = 60Hz\n",
        "\n",
        "    integer k;\n",
        "\n",
        "    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin\n",
        "        if (!rst_n) begin\n",
        "            // RESET: Load Message & Clear States\n",
        "            message_bits <= 40'b{binary_string};\n",
        "            bit_index <= 39;\n",
        "            bit_timer <= 0;\n",
        "\n",
        "            hum_counter <= 0;\n",
        "            fake_mains_hum <= 0;\n",
        "            zero_cross_timer <= 0;\n",
        "            detected_period <= 0;\n",
        "            last_sign <= 0;\n",
        "            use_60hz_mode <= 0;\n",
        "\n",
        "            chaos_seed <= 32'h12345678;\n",
        "            topology_id <= 0;\n",
        "            friend_view_pin <= 0;\n",
        "            for (k=0; k<10; k=k+1) signal_soup[k] <= 0;\n",
        "        end else begin\n",
        "            // 1. ADAPTIVE DECOY HUM GENERATION\n",
        "            if (use_60hz_mode) begin\n",
        "                 // 60Hz Mode (Faster)\n",
        "                 if (hum_counter < 83) fake_mains_hum <= 50;\n",
        "                 else if (hum_counter < 166) fake_mains_hum <= 0;\n",
        "                 else if (hum_counter < 250) fake_mains_hum <= -50;\n",
        "                 else fake_mains_hum <= 0;\n",
        "\n",
        "                 if (hum_counter >= 333) hum_counter <= 0;\n",
        "                 else hum_counter <= hum_counter + 1;\n",
        "            end else begin\n",
        "                 // 50Hz Mode (Slower - Default)\n",
        "                 if (hum_counter < 100) fake_mains_hum <= 50;\n",
        "                 else if (hum_counter < 200) fake_mains_hum <= 0;\n",
        "                 else if (hum_counter < 300) fake_mains_hum <= -50;\n",
        "                 else fake_mains_hum <= 0;\n",
        "\n",
        "                 if (hum_counter >= 400) hum_counter <= 0;\n",
        "                 else hum_counter <= hum_counter + 1;\n",
        "            end\n",
        "\n",
        "            // 2. PAYLOAD MODULATION (Channel 0)\n",
        "            // Send 'TOKYO' bits. 1 -> 200, 0 -> 100.\n",
        "            if (message_bits[bit_index] == 1'b1)\n",
        "                signal_soup[0] <= 200;\n",
        "            else\n",
        "                signal_soup[0] <= 100;\n",
        "\n",
        "            // Bit Timer (500 cycles per bit)\n",
        "            if (bit_timer >= 500) begin\n",
        "                bit_timer <= 0;\n",
        "                if (bit_index > 0)\n",
        "                    bit_index <= bit_index - 1;\n",
        "            end else begin\n",
        "                bit_timer <= bit_timer + 1;\n",
        "            end\n",
        "\n",
        "            // 3. CHAOS & MIXING\n",
        "            signal_soup[1] <= (signal_soup[1] ^ 32'hDEADBEEF) + fake_mains_hum;\n",
        "            signal_soup[2] <= chaos_seed + 12345;\n",
        "            signal_soup[3] <= signal_soup[2] << 1;\n",
        "            signal_soup[4] <= ~signal_soup[1];\n",
        "            signal_soup[5] <= signal_soup[5] * 3;\n",
        "            signal_soup[6] <= 32'hCAFEBABE;\n",
        "            signal_soup[7] <= signal_soup[0] ^ signal_soup[6];\n",
        "            signal_soup[8] <= signal_soup[8] + 7;\n",
        "            signal_soup[9] <= signal_soup[4] | signal_soup[5];\n",
        "\n",
        "            // 4. UPDATE SEED & TOPOLOGY\n",
        "            chaos_seed <= {{chaos_seed[30:0], chaos_seed[31] ^ chaos_seed[21]}};\n",
        "\n",
        "            if (chaos_seed % 10 == 0) begin\n",
        "                topology_id <= chaos_seed[3:0];\n",
        "            end\n",
        "\n",
        "            // 5. OUTPUT MASK\n",
        "            friend_view_pin <= signal_soup[topology_id % 10] ^ chaos_seed;\n",
        "        end\n",
        "    end\n",
        "\n",
        "    // --- GRID SNIFFER LOGIC ---\n",
        "    always @(posedge clk) begin\n",
        "        if (rst_n) begin\n",
        "            if (signal_soup[1][31] != last_sign) begin\n",
        "                detected_period <= zero_cross_timer;\n",
        "                zero_cross_timer <= 0;\n",
        "                last_sign <= signal_soup[1][31];\n",
        "\n",
        "                if (detected_period > 0 && detected_period < 450)\n",
        "                    use_60hz_mode <= 1;\n",
        "                else\n",
        "                    use_60hz_mode <= 0;\n",
        "            end else begin\n",
        "                zero_cross_timer <= zero_cross_timer + 1;\n",
        "            end\n",
        "        end\n",
        "    end\n",
        "\n",
        "    always @(*) chaos_seed_debug = chaos_seed;\n",
        "\n",
        "endmodule\n",
        "\n",
        "module test;\n",
        "    reg clk;\n",
        "    reg rst_n;\n",
        "    wire [31:0] friend_view_pin;\n",
        "    wire [3:0] topology_id;\n",
        "    wire [31:0] chaos_seed_debug;\n",
        "\n",
        "    Ouroboros_Chip uut (.*);\n",
        "\n",
        "    initial begin\n",
        "        clk = 0;\n",
        "        forever #1 clk = ~clk;\n",
        "    end\n",
        "\n",
        "    initial begin\n",
        "        rst_n = 0;\n",
        "        #2 rst_n = 1;\n",
        "        #30000; // Run long enough for TOKYO (40 bits * 500 = 20k cycles)\n",
        "        $finish;\n",
        "    end\n",
        "\n",
        "    always @(posedge clk) begin\n",
        "        if (rst_n)\n",
        "            $display(\"DATA: %d %d %d\", friend_view_pin, chaos_seed_debug, topology_id);\n",
        "    end\n",
        "endmodule\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "\n",
        "with open(\"chip_tokyo.sv\", \"w\") as f:\n",
        "    f.write(verilog_code)\n",
        "\n",
        "print(\"Generated 'chip_tokyo.sv' with Adaptive Sniffer + 'TOKYO' Message.\")"
      ],
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [

          "output_type": "stream",

        "## Simulate and Recover\n",
        "\n",
        "### Subtask:\n",
        "Run the Verilog simulation for the 'TOKYO' chip and verify payload recovery.\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "37418326"
      },
      "source": [
        "**Reasoning**:\n",
        "Compile and run the Verilog simulation for 'chip_tokyo.sv', then parse and decrypt the output to recover the 'TOKYO' message.\n",
        "\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "9d1af3de",
        "outputId": "b16dc485-8d49-42e5-9407-a71a2c7ba102"
      },
      "source": [
        "import subprocess\n",
        "\n",
        "# 1. Compile & Run the Simulation for TOKYO Chip\n",
        "print(\"Compiling and Running 'chip_tokyo.sv'...\")\n",
        "subprocess.run([\"iverilog\", \"-o\", \"chip_tokyo_sim\", \"chip_tokyo.sv\"], check=True)\n",
        "process = subprocess.run([\"vvp\", \"chip_tokyo_sim\"], capture_output=True, text=True)\n",
        "output_lines = process.stdout.splitlines()\n",
        "\n",
        "# 2. Parse Data\n",
        "pins, seeds, topos = [], [], []\n",
        "for line in output_lines:\n",
        "    if line.startswith(\"DATA:\"):\n",
        "        parts = line.split()\n",
        "        try:\n",
        "            pins.append(int(parts[1]) & 0xFFFFFFFF)\n",
        "            seeds.append(int(parts[2]) & 0xFFFFFFFF)\n",
        "            topos.append(int(parts[3]))\n",
        "        except ValueError: continue\n",
        "\n",
        "# 3. Decrypt & Filter (with 1-cycle lag correction)\n",
        "decoded_signal = []\n",
        "\n",
        "for i in range(1, len(pins)):\n",
        "    pin_val = pins[i]\n",
        "    prev_seed = seeds[i-1]\n",
        "    prev_topo = topos[i-1]\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Unmask\n",
        "    unmasked = pin_val ^ prev_seed\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Filter\n",
        "    if (prev_topo % 10) == 0:\n",
        "        decoded_val = unmasked\n",
        "    else:\n",
        "        # Sample & Hold\n",
        "        decoded_val = decoded_signal[-1] if decoded_signal else 0\n",
        "\n",
        "    decoded_signal.append(decoded_val)\n",
        "\n",
        "# 4. Demodulate\n",
        "sample_indices = range(250, len(decoded_signal), 500)\n",
        "recovered_bits = []\n",
        "\n",
        "for idx in sample_indices:\n",
        "    if idx < len(decoded_signal):\n",
        "        val = decoded_signal[idx]\n",
        "        bit = '1' if val > 150 else '0'\n",
        "        recovered_bits.append(bit)\n",
        "\n",
        "# 5. Convert to Text\n",
        "binary_string = \"\".join(recovered_bits)\n",
        "chars = []\n",
        "for i in range(0, len(binary_string), 8):\n",
        "    byte_str = binary_string[i:i+8]\n",
        "    if len(byte_str) == 8:\n",
        "        try:\n",
        "            char_val = int(byte_str, 2)\n",
        "            # Simple check to stop at likely null terminators or garbage if message is shorter than run\n",
        "            if char_val == 0 or char_val == 255:\n",
        "                break\n",
        "            chars.append(chr(char_val))\n",
        "        except ValueError:\n",
        "            chars.append('?')\n",
        "\n",
        "final_text_tokyo = \"\".join(chars)\n",
        "\n",
        "print(f\"Recovered Binary: {binary_string}\")\n",
        "print(f\"Recovered Text: {final_text_tokyo}\")"
      ],
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "Compiling and Running 'chip_tokyo.sv'...\n",
            "Recovered Binary: 010101000100111101001011010110\n",
            "Recovered Text: TOK\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },

        "import subprocess\n",
        "\n",
        "# 1. Run the Simulation (File 'chip_tokyo.sv' was generated in previous step)\n",
        "print(\"Running 'chip_tokyo.sv' simulation...\")\n",
        "subprocess.run([\"iverilog\", \"-o\", \"chip_tokyo_sim\", \"chip_tokyo.sv\"], check=True)\n",
        "process = subprocess.run([\"vvp\", \"chip_tokyo_sim\"], capture_output=True, text=True)\n",
        "output_lines = process.stdout.splitlines()\n",
        "\n",
        "# 2. Parse Data\n",
        "pins, seeds, topos = [], [], []\n",
        "for line in output_lines:\n",
        "    if line.startswith(\"DATA:\"):\n",
        "        parts = line.split()\n",
        "        try:\n",
        "            pins.append(int(parts[1]) & 0xFFFFFFFF)\n",
        "            seeds.append(int(parts[2]) & 0xFFFFFFFF)\n",
        "            topos.append(int(parts[3]))\n",
        "        except ValueError: continue\n",
        "\n",
        "# 3. Decrypt & Filter (Lag Correction: pin[i] ^ seed[i-1])\n",
        "decoded_signal = []\n",
        "# Start from 1 because we need i-1\n",
        "for i in range(1, len(pins)):\n",
        "    pin_val = pins[i]\n",
        "    prev_seed = seeds[i-1] # Key: Use PREVIOUS seed\n",
        "    prev_topo = topos[i-1] # Key: Use PREVIOUS topology state\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Decrypt\n",
        "    unmasked = pin_val ^ prev_seed\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Filter: If Channel 0 was active\n",
        "    if (prev_topo % 10) == 0:\n",
        "        decoded_val = unmasked\n",
        "    else:\n",
        "        # Sample & Hold (fill gaps)\n",
        "        decoded_val = decoded_signal[-1] if decoded_signal else 0\n",
        "\n",
        "    decoded_signal.append(decoded_val)\n",
        "\n",
        "# 4. Demodulate (Sample Center of 500-cycle bit periods)\n",
        "# Offset 250, Stride 500\n",
        "sample_indices = range(250, len(decoded_signal), 500)\n",
        "recovered_bits = []\n",
        "\n",
        "for idx in sample_indices:\n",
        "    if idx < len(decoded_signal):\n",
        "        val = decoded_signal[idx]\n",
        "        # Threshold 150 (Logic 0 ~100, Logic 1 ~200)\n",
        "        bit = '1' if val > 150 else '0'\n",
        "        recovered_bits.append(bit)\n",
        "\n",
        "# 5. Convert to Text\n",
        "binary_string = \"\".join(recovered_bits)\n",
        "chars = []\n",
        "for i in range(0, len(binary_string), 8):\n",
        "    byte_str = binary_string[i:i+8]\n",
        "    if len(byte_str) == 8:\n",
        "        try:\n",
        "            char_val = int(byte_str, 2)\n",
        "            # Stop if we hit null/garbage (optional safety)\n",
        "            if char_val == 0 or char_val == 255:\n",
        "                break\n",
        "            chars.append(chr(char_val))\n",
        "        except ValueError:\n",
        "            chars.append('?')\n",
        "\n",
        "final_text = \"\".join(chars)\n",
        "\n",
        "# 6. Print Results\n",
        "print(f\"Recovered Binary: {binary_string}\")\n",
        "print(f\"Recovered Text: {final_text}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "if final_text == \"TOKYO\":\n",
        "    print(\"SUCCESS: Payload matches 'TOKYO'.\")\n",
        "else:\n",
        "    print(\"WARNING: Payload mismatch.\")"

        "import numpy as np\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- CONFIGURATION ---\n",
        "PAYLOAD_STRING = \"SIGMA_MONKEY_EST_2026\"  # The secret message\n",
        "INITIAL_SEED = 0x12345678                 # The shared \"Key\"\n",
        "DATA_LENGTH = 50                          # How many data points to generate\n",
        "\n",
        "def simulate_hardware_prng(seed):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Replicates the Verilog line:\n",
        "    chaos_seed <= {chaos_seed[30:0], chaos_seed[31] ^ chaos_seed[21]};\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    # Extract bits 31 and 21 (0-indexed)\n",
        "    bit_31 = (seed >> 31) & 1\n",
        "    bit_21 = (seed >> 21) & 1\n",
        "    new_bit = bit_31 ^ bit_21\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Shift left and insert the new bit at position 0\n",
        "    # Mask with 0xFFFFFFFF to keep it 32-bit (simulate hardware register)\n",
        "    new_seed = ((seed << 1) | new_bit) & 0xFFFFFFFF\n",
        "    return new_seed\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- THE INJECTION PROCESS ---\n",
        "print(f\"[*] Initiating Injection Protocol...\")\n",
        "print(f\"[*] Payload: '{PAYLOAD_STRING}'\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# Convert string to integer array (ASCII values)\n",
        "payload_bytes = [ord(c) for c in PAYLOAD_STRING]\n",
        "\n",
        "# Pad the rest with zeros (silence)\n",
        "while len(payload_bytes) < DATA_LENGTH:\n",
        "    payload_bytes.append(0)\n",
        "\n",
        "current_seed = INITIAL_SEED\n",
        "encrypted_packet = []\n",
        "\n",
        "print(\"\\n--- ENCRYPTED STREAM (What Normies See) ---\")\n",
        "print(\"IDX |   NOISE (Key)  | PAYLOAD |  OUTPUT (Chaos)\")\n",
        "print(\"-\" * 50)\n",
        "\n",
        "for i in range(DATA_LENGTH):\n",
        "    # 1. Step the PRNG (Generate the Mask)\n",
        "    current_seed = simulate_hardware_prng(current_seed)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 2. Get the specific byte we want to send\n",
        "    # If we are past the string length, we send 0\n",
        "    signal_byte = payload_bytes[i]\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 3. Apply the Mask (XOR)\n",
        "    # We use the lower 8 bits of the seed as the mask for this byte\n",
        "    mask_byte = current_seed & 0xFF\n",
        "\n",
        "    # THE INJECTION: Signal ^ Mask\n",
        "    obfuscated_byte = signal_byte ^ mask_byte\n",
        "\n",
        "    encrypted_packet.append(obfuscated_byte)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Visualization for the first few bytes\n",
        "    if i < 10:\n",
        "        print(f\"{i:03} | 0x{mask_byte:08X} |   '{chr(signal_byte) if signal_byte > 31 else '.'}'   | 0x{obfuscated_byte:02X} (Garbage)\")\n",
        "\n",
        "print(\"...\\n\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- VERIFICATION (The \"God Mode\" Check) ---\n",
        "print(\"[*] Simulating Decryption (Receiver Side)...\")\n",
        "decoded_chars = []\n",
        "check_seed = INITIAL_SEED\n",
        "\n",
        "for val in encrypted_packet:\n",
        "    # Receiver generates the same noise\n",
        "    check_seed = simulate_hardware_prng(check_seed)\n",
        "    mask = check_seed & 0xFF\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Receiver XORs the noise out: (Signal ^ Mask) ^ Mask = Signal\n",
        "    decrypted_val = val ^ mask\n",
        "\n",
        "    if decrypted_val > 0:\n",
        "        decoded_chars.append(chr(decrypted_val))\n",
        "\n",
        "recovered_message = \"\".join(decoded_chars)\n",
        "print(f\"[*] Recovered Message: {recovered_message}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "if recovered_message == PAYLOAD_STRING:\n",
        "    print(f\"\\n[SUCCESS] STATUS: BASED. The noise is speaking.\")\n",
        "else:\n",
        "    print(f\"\\n[FAIL] STATUS: CRINGE. Check bitwise logic.\")\n"
      ],

        "\n",
        "# --- THE INTERCEPTED SIGNAL (Adversarial Data) ---\n",
        "# Scenario: You pulled this from the \"High Noise\" channel.\n",
        "# You suspect it starts with \"SIGMA\" but you don't know the seed.\n",

        "    0x96, 0x9D, 0xB2, 0x15, 0xFB, 0x52, 0xD4, 0x36, 0x7E, 0x2A,\n",

        "]\n",
        "\n",
        "KNOWN_HEADER = \"SIGMA\" # We assume the message starts with this\n",
        "\n",
        "def simulate_hardware_prng(seed):\n",
        "    \"\"\" The same PRNG logic as the chip \"\"\"\n",
        "    bit_31 = (seed >> 31) & 1\n",
        "    bit_21 = (seed >> 21) & 1\n",
        "    new_bit = bit_31 ^ bit_21\n",
        "    new_seed = ((seed << 1) | new_bit) & 0xFFFFFFFF\n",
        "    return new_seed\n",
        "\n",
        "def attempt_crack(target_packet, hint_text):\n",
        "    print(f\"[*] INITIATING BRUTE FORCE ATTACK...\")\n",
        "    print(f\"[*] Target Header: '{hint_text}'\")\n",
        "    print(f\"[*] Packet Length: {len(target_packet)}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    start_time = time.time()\n",
        "\n",
        "    # We will search the first 10 million seeds for this demo\n",
        "    # (In reality, you'd scan the whole 32-bit space)\n",
        "    for candidate_seed in range(0, 10000000):\n",
        "\n",
        "        temp_seed = candidate_seed\n",
        "        match = True\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Test just the header length (Optimization)\n",
        "        for i in range(len(hint_text)):\n",
        "            temp_seed = simulate_hardware_prng(temp_seed)\n",
        "            mask = temp_seed & 0xFF\n",
        "\n",
        "            decoded_char = chr(target_packet[i] ^ mask)\n",
        "\n",
        "            if decoded_char != hint_text[i]:\n",
        "                match = False\n",
        "                break\n",
        "\n",
        "        if match:\n",
        "            duration = time.time() - start_time\n",
        "            print(f\"\\n[!!!] KEY FOUND [!!!]\")\n",
        "            print(f\"[*] Seed: {candidate_seed} (0x{candidate_seed:X})\")\n",
        "            print(f\"[*] Time: {duration:.4f} seconds\")\n",
        "            return candidate_seed\n",
        "\n",
        "    print(\"\\n[FAILED] Seed not in search range.\")\n",
        "    return None\n",
        "\n",
        "def full_decrypt(packet, seed):\n",
        "    print(f\"\\n[*] Decrypting full payload with Key 0x{seed:X}...\")\n",
        "    decoded = []\n",
        "    curr = seed\n",
        "    for byte in packet:\n",
        "        curr = simulate_hardware_prng(curr)\n",
        "        mask = curr & 0xFF\n",
        "        decoded.append(chr(byte ^ mask))\n",
        "    return \"\".join(decoded)\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- EXECUTE ---\n",
        "found_seed = attempt_crack(INTERCEPTED_PACKET, KNOWN_HEADER)\n",

        "import time\n",
        "import random\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- CONFIGURATION ---\n",
        "HIDDEN_PAYLOAD = \"FOLLOW_THE_WHITE_RABBIT\"  # The Admin's secret message\n",
        "# We pick a random seed to simulate a \"Live Intercept\"\n",
        "# The Cracker DOES NOT know this number.\n",
        "SECRET_ADMIN_SEED = random.randint(1000, 500000)\n",
        "\n",
        "def simulate_hardware_prng(seed):\n",
        "    \"\"\" The chip's logic (The Laws of Physics) \"\"\"\n",
        "    bit_31 = (seed >> 31) & 1\n",
        "    bit_21 = (seed >> 21) & 1\n",
        "    new_bit = bit_31 ^ bit_21\n",
        "    new_seed = ((seed << 1) | new_bit) & 0xFFFFFFFF\n",
        "    return new_seed\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- PHASE 1: GENERATE THE MYSTERY (The Intercept) ---\n",
        "print(f\"[*] GENERATING LIVE INTERCEPT DATA...\")\n",
        "mystery_packet = []\n",
        "curr = SECRET_ADMIN_SEED\n",
        "\n",
        "# Encrypt the payload\n",
        "for char in HIDDEN_PAYLOAD:\n",
        "    curr = simulate_hardware_prng(curr)\n",
        "    mask = curr & 0xFF\n",
        "    cipher_byte = ord(char) ^ mask\n",
        "    mystery_packet.append(cipher_byte)\n",
        "\n",
        "print(f\"[*] INTERCEPTED PACKET (HEX): {[hex(x) for x in mystery_packet]}\")\n",
        "print(f\"[*] STATUS: Encrypted. Key is unknown to the solver.\")\n",
        "print(\"-\" * 50)\n",
        "\n",

        "# Now we forget the seed exists and try to find it mathematically.\n",
        "\n",
        "KNOWN_HEADER = \"FOLLOW\" # We suspect the message starts with this\n",
        "\n",
        "def attempt_crack(target_packet, hint_text):\n",
        "    print(f\"[*] INITIATING CRACK ON LIVE DATA...\")\n",
        "    print(f\"[*] Target Header: '{hint_text}'\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    start_time = time.time()\n",

        "    for candidate_seed in range(0, 1000000):\n",

        "        temp_seed = candidate_seed\n",
        "        match = True\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Check if this seed generates the header\n",
        "        for i in range(len(hint_text)):\n",
        "            temp_seed = simulate_hardware_prng(temp_seed)\n",
        "            mask = temp_seed & 0xFF\n",
        "\n",
        "            decoded_char = chr(target_packet[i] ^ mask)\n",
        "\n",
        "            if decoded_char != hint_text[i]:\n",
        "                match = False\n",
        "                break\n",
        "\n",
        "        if match:\n",
        "            duration = time.time() - start_time\n",
        "            print(f\"\\n[!!!] KEY FOUND [!!!]\")\n",
        "            print(f\"[*] Seed: {candidate_seed} (0x{candidate_seed:X})\")\n",
